summary: Dynamic Health IT opposes the removal of the 170.315(b)(1) and (b)(2) certification criteria, arguing that without these or a replacement FHIR certification, interoperability will suffer. They highlight the importance of the C-CDA Validation Tool and the lack of current FHIR certification requirements to ensure bi-directional data exchange.
key_arguments: Removing (b)(1) and (b)(2) will harm healthcare interoperability.
No current certification exists to receive and ingest TOC FHIR data as a replacement.
The C-CDA Validation Tool is critical to ensure interoperable C-CDAs.
Without these requirements, EHR vendors may stop generating or produce faulty C-CDAs.
recommendations: Maintain the 170.315(b)(1) and (b)(2) certification criteria or establish a replacement FHIR certification criterion.
Ensure certification requirements exist to support bi-directional FHIR data exchange for TOC.
notable_quote: Without a replacement requirement for EHRs to support bi-directional FHIR data, this leaves a big gap in healthcare interoperability.
